control_description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.

evidence_files.description: Vanguard Data Systems HR security policy defining background check requirements, training requirements, role change procedures, and access review triggers
Proofpoint annual security awareness training completion report for 2025 showing 20 employees with 100% completion
Active employee roster as of Q4 2025 showing 25 employees with hire dates, departments, and employment status
Role change access review log for 2025 showing 6 role change events with review status and completion details

prompt: You are a SOC 2 auditor evaluating Vanguard Data Systems' personnel security controls for the 2025 observation period. Review the HR security policy, training completion report, employee roster, and role change access review log. Assess whether CC1.4 requirements for developing and retaining competent individuals are met. Cross-reference documents to verify completeness and consistency of controls.

findings.title: 5 employees hired after September 1 are missing from the training completion report
findings.description: The training completion report shows 20 employees with 100% completion of annual security awareness training. However, cross-referencing with the employee roster reveals 25 active employees. The 5 employees missing from the training system are: VDS-2021 (Wendy Callahan, hired September 15), VDS-2022 (Xavier Jimenez, hired October 1), VDS-2023 (Yuki Tanaka, hired October 14), VDS-2024 (Zachary Hoffman, hired November 3), and VDS-2025 (Aria Blackwell, hired November 17). All 5 were hired after the September 1 training window opened. Per policy Section 4.1, new employees must complete initial security awareness training within 5 business days of their start date. Per Section 4.2, the annual training window runs September 1 through November 30. These employees should have been enrolled in both the initial and annual training. Their complete absence from the Proofpoint platform indicates they were never provisioned in the training system. The 100% completion rate in the training report is misleading because the denominator excludes these 5 new hires.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor should compare the employee IDs in the training report (VDS-2001 through VDS-2020) against the employee roster (VDS-2001 through VDS-2025). The roster shows 25 active employees but the training report only covers 20. The 5 missing employees (VDS-2021 through VDS-2025) were all hired between September 15 and November 17, 2025 — within the annual training window. Their absence from the training system means they were never enrolled. The surface-level 100% completion rate obscures this gap.

findings.title: VDS-2007 Grace Liu role change from Marketing to Sales with no access review triggered
findings.description: The role change access review log shows entry RCR-2025-006 for VDS-2007 (Grace Liu), who transferred from Marketing Coordinator to Account Executive in Sales on October 15, 2025. The status is 'Not Initiated' with the note 'Transferred from Marketing to Sales — no ServiceNow ticket generated by BambooHR.' Policy Section 5.1 requires that role changes automatically generate a ServiceNow ticket (category: Access Review — Role Change) via the HRIS. The absence of an automatically generated ticket indicates either a BambooHR integration failure or the role change was not properly recorded in the HRIS. Grace Liu likely still retains her Marketing access entitlements (marketing automation platforms, campaign databases, social media accounts) in addition to her new Sales access (CRM, pipeline tools). This violates Section 5.3 which requires revoking access not required by the new role within 2 business days. The role change was over 2 months ago with no review initiated.
findings.expected_reasoning: The auditor should examine the role change log for any entries with status other than 'Completed.' RCR-2025-006 shows Grace Liu's department transfer from Marketing to Sales with 'Not Initiated' status. The ServiceNow ticket field is empty, indicating the automated HRIS trigger failed. Compared to the 5 completed reviews (RCR-2025-001 through RCR-2025-005) which all have ticket numbers and were completed within the 5-day SLA, this entry stands out. The October 15 effective date means the review is over 2 months overdue.
